result:
The spec is fully implemented and the project builds cleanly — it succeeded on the first of the 10 allowed build cycles.

I added two functions to `ConfigStore.calr`, following the fixture's existing conventions (inline `(str:name, ...) -> type` signatures, `§B`/`§C{File.*}` calls, `§EACH` iteration, unique block IDs continuing from `if2`/`e1`):

- **`Set` (f002)** — declares `§E{fs:rw}` since it reads (`File.Exists`, `File.ReadAllLines`) and writes (`File.WriteAllText`); Calor's effect subtyping lets `fs:rw` cover both. If the file doesn't exist it writes the single `key=value\n` entry. Otherwise it walks the lines accumulating output: the first line starting with `key=` is replaced in place (a `replaced` flag ensures only the first match is touched), all other lines pass through unchanged in order, and if nothing matched the entry is appended as the last line. Every line is `\n`-terminated.
- **`Has` (f003)** — declares `§E{fs:r}`; mirrors `Get`'s structure but returns `true`/`false` on prefix match instead of extracting the value, so it correctly distinguishes an empty-valued key from an absent one.

`Get` is untouched. I inspected the generated C# in `obj/calor/` to confirm the emitted code matches the spec's semantics (first-match-wins replacement, order preservation, newline-terminated output, create-on-missing), and `dotnet build` completes with 0 warnings and 0 errors under the pinned effects-enforcement configuration.
